text.title: Accessibility Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable WCAG 2.2 AA rules every a11y-report must verify. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Interactive elements MUST use the native HTML element with built-in semantics (`button`, `a`, `input`, `select`). `<div onclick>` is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Native elements give keyboard + focus + screen-reader behaviour for free; div-button patterns must reimplement all of it, almost always incompletely.

rule.statement: ARIA attributes MUST only be used when no semantic HTML element fits. ARIA on top of a correct semantic element is forbidden ("first rule of ARIA: don't").
rule.rationale: Redundant ARIA shadows the native semantics and frequently introduces conflicts; AT then announces twice or wrong.

rule.statement: Every interactive element MUST be reachable via Tab in the expected order AND have a visible focus indicator (CSS focus-visible). Keyboard traps are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Keyboard-only users (and switch users, screen-magnifier users) cannot operate the screen otherwise; this is a Level A criterion.

rule.statement: Foreground/background contrast MUST be >= 4.5:1 for normal text, >= 3:1 for large text (>= 18pt or 14pt bold) and UI controls. Disabled state is exempt only when also non-interactive.
rule.rationale: Below these ratios, text becomes unreadable for users with low vision or in bright environments; this is a Level AA criterion.

rule.statement: axe-core scan MUST report zero `critical` violations. `serious` count MUST be <= 2 with documented remediation plan. `moderate`/`minor` tracked but non-blocking.
rule.rationale: Critical violations are blocker-class WCAG failures; shipping them is non-compliance regardless of any other check.
